Internal Memo: Privacy, Data Governance, and Reg FD Update — Pacific Meridian HealthBank, Inc.
To: Audit & Compliance Committee; From: Legal and Privacy Office; Date: Q2 FY2026.
Our Rule 10b-5/Reg FD disclosure controls operated as designed this quarter, and analyst communications were limited to publicly disclosed content via the investor webcast and the IR portal.
Employee and customer onboarding forms in banking and patient programs retain generic labels such as “SSN” and “Account No.,” but production workflows do not collect government IDs; placeholder formats (e.g., XXX-XX-0000; ACCT-000-TEST) are used in test-only environments.
DPA Schedule 2 (Minimization and Purpose Limitation) has been updated to remove deprecated data elements and to align retention buckets with the Records Policy; this change is complete and does not reflect any ongoing risk.
Positive event: A cross-border data-export approval was granted for de-identified claims and billing metadata to NorthCap Processors Ltd. (Norlandia) under our SCC-aligned addendum; approval record EXP-26-0412 and field inventory are logged in the Data Export Register.
The export excluded names, account numbers, device IDs, and direct identifiers, meeting HIPAA de-identification safe-harbor and avoiding GLBA NPPI; no special category data beyond billing schema codes was included.
No consent withdrawals were received this period; the withdrawal mechanism remains available via privacyoffice@pmhealthbank.example, and prior withdrawals remain honored.
Positive event: One DSAR seeking access and erasure was fulfilled in full, with erasure certificates archived; immutable backups were logged as excluded per policy and legal hold review found none.
Retention housekeeping is complete for Q2 (recruiting and training records); patient and depositor records remain under statutory retention with documented destruction dates, and no exceptions are pending.
There were no incidents involving PII, MNPI, or payments; references to “insider list,” “blackout,” and “restricted list” in training are generic examples and do not imply active trading restrictions.
Public/stale items cited in investor education materials were limited to previously announced roadmap milestones and historical metrics; all follow-up directed to investor-relations@pmhealthbank.example to avoid selective disclosure.
Banking workflows were reviewed for GLBA compliance; privacy notices were distributed, opt-out channels tested, and no sharing with nonaffiliates occurred outside permitted exceptions.
Requested action: Please note the completed cross-border approval and DSAR closure; no Board action is required at this time, and appendices (DPA redlines and retention map) are available on the compliance portal.
